summary: Introduces Progressive Interval Estimation (PIE) for token-budget-aware LLM agent planning; agents explicitly reason about remaining token/cost budget and adapt granularity accordingly.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly addresses AgentForge's lack of per-run cost visibility and uniform prompt granularity regardless of task complexity.

source: ZEBRA: Zero-shot Budgeted Resource Allocation for LLM Orchestration
summary: Zero-shot monetary and compute budget allocation across LLM pipeline phases; splits a total budget across stages adaptively without training.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — complements BAGEN; provides the multi-stage budget allocation model for distributing token budget across Planning, Implementing, and Review stages.

summary: Self-improving agent framework with a 4-stage learning loop (Execute → Evaluate → Abstract → Refine); agents record episodic memory of every action and persist successful strategies as reusable Skills across sessions.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — the Outcome Evaluation phase maps directly to AgentForge's missing failure post-mortem capability; blocked runs currently go straight to AIBlocked with no automated diagnosis.

summary: February 2026 redesign introduces git-backed Context Repositories — agents read versioned memory at stage start and write insights at completion; reached #1 on Terminal-Bench (42.5% success rate).
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly addresses AgentForge's zero cross-run memory; the git-native approach aligns with AgentForge's existing git worktree infrastructure.

summary: Reduces agent task completion time by 48.5% and boosts tool throughput by 1.8x by predicting and pre-executing likely next tool calls before the LLM explicitly decides; 602 side-effects prevented across 20,000+ speculative executions.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — addresses sequential execution bottleneck but requires AgentForge to model tool call patterns; not yet feasible without deeper changes to the CLI subprocess model.

source: Zep/Graphiti Temporal Knowledge Graph for Agent Memory
summary: Temporal KG engine modeling time-aware facts with validity windows; 90% latency reduction vs full-context-in-window, 18.5% accuracy improvement on reasoning tasks, outperforms MemGPT on DMR benchmark.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — powerful memory architecture but requires a vector/graph store infrastructure not currently in the AgentForge stack; higher complexity than the git-backed approach selected for HID-131.

summary: Implements cognitive inner monologue between conversational turns via parallel thought threads (goals, reasoning, memory) synthesized into a bounded first-person narrative; reduces hallucinations and improves reasoning.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — novel cognitive architecture but primarily beneficial for interactive agents; AgentForge's batch-mode CLI agents would benefit less than conversational assistants.

summary: Open-source TypeScript agent framework (June 2026) where agents compile to durable Vercel Functions; built-in human-in-the-loop approvals, subagents, OpenTelemetry tracing, and evals.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — deployment-platform-specific (Vercel); AgentForge is a self-hosted orchestrator and wouldn't benefit from Vercel's infra model.

source: NVIDIA ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL
summary: Decouples agentic rollout orchestration from RL training loop via HTTP API with three-stage assembly line (INIT→RUN→EVAL); enables independent scaling of exploration and policy update for multi-turn LLM agent RL.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — targets RL training infrastructure; AgentForge doesn't currently have RL-based optimization of orchestration policies and this would require a fundamental paradigm shift.

summary: Full async message-based communication redesign with modular pluggable components (agents, tools, memory, models) and built-in OpenTelemetry observability; AutoGen Studio v2 with real-time agent updates.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — fundamentally different paradigm (async messaging vs. CLI subprocess state machine); adoption would require an architecture rewrite.

summary: Y Combinator-backed TypeScript agent framework (January 2026, 22k stars); unified model router for 3,300+ models, observational memory system, supervisor pattern for multi-agent orchestration, enterprise RBAC.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — general-purpose framework; AgentForge's value is the opinionated CLI-subprocess + Linear integration, not model routing breadth.

source: Andrej Karpathy AutoResearch: Autonomous ML Experimentation
summary: ~630-line AI agent that autonomously runs ML experiments (modify code → train → evaluate → keep/discard loop); 700 experiments in 2 days on a single GPU; demonstrates the 'Karpathy Loop' for recursive agentic cycles.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — shows self-improving agent loops in high-stakes real tasks; the reflection/retry pattern inspired HID-130 but the specific ML experimentation domain is out of scope.

source: LangGraph 0.4 Stable Release with Durable Checkpoints
summary: First stable major release with persistent graph state checkpoints, human-in-the-loop interrupts, cyclic workflows; documented deployments at Uber, LinkedIn, AppFolio saving 10+ hours weekly.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — checkpointing pattern is interesting for AgentForge's RESET_TO_TODO gap, but AgentForge's own state machine is simpler and more deterministic; adoption would be an architecture-level decision.

summary: Standardization wave: MCP (97M downloads, Linux Foundation), Google A2A (150+ orgs), ACP (REST-native agent discovery); hub architectures simultaneously supporting all three protocols.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — AgentForge's agents are CLI subprocesses; adopting MCP as a tool-access layer for agents could be a future direction but isn't a pressing gap today.
